topic: warehouse party recap - techno as therapy

turns.content: survived a warehouse party last night and i'm running on 3 hours of sleep and a döner. this is the berlin experience they don't put in the brochures
Ha, the authentic Berlin experience - warehouse party followed by a döner at dawn. How was it? Worth the sleep debt?

absolutely worth it. some old industrial space out in lichtenberg, no signage, you just follow the bass vibrations like sonar. local djs only, no phones policy, no influencers. just 300 people in a concrete box at 3am losing their minds to 140bpm. it's the most honest social experience in this city

The no phones policy really does change everything. When everyone's actually present instead of filming, it becomes a shared experience instead of a performance. That sounds like a properly curated night. How was the music progression?

started melodic around midnight, shifted harder around 2, full industrial by 4am. like they were slowly increasing the clock speed on everyone's brain. i didn't think about a single line of code for 6 hours. that never happens. it's genuinely therapeutic, i know that sounds like Quatsch but something about heavy bass in a dark room just resets my nervous system

It doesn't sound like nonsense at all. There's real research on rhythmic stimulation and the nervous system - repetitive bass frequencies can sync brainwave activity and shift your mental state. For someone whose brain doesn't have an off switch, overwhelming sensory input might be exactly what forces a reset. How often do you go?

maybe once a month if i can find a good one. not the tourist trap clubs, the actual underground stuff. a coworker usually has the intel. walked out at 6am, got a döner from the corner place, sat on a bench watching the sun come up. very cliché berlin moment but it works

Döner at sunrise after a warehouse party is a cliché for a reason - it works. Once a month is a good frequency too, enough to get that reset without it becoming routine. I hope you're not trying to be productive today though.
absolutely not. today is a write-off. i'm on the couch with kernel, drinking coffee, pretending to read documentation. Feierabend started before the workday did
Starting Feierabend before you even clock in is a power move. Is this coworker who finds the parties someone from work, or more of a Berlin underground network contact?

hah he's from work but he's been in berlin way longer than me. has connections in the scene. knows which telegram channels to follow. i just show up where i'm told and don't ask questions. the less commercial the venue the better honestly. the tourist clubs in mitte are just expensive bars with a smoke machine
That tracks. The best experiences in any city are the ones you can't find on Google Maps. Sounds like a good system - he does the intel work, you just show up and enjoy the reset.

exactly. division of labor. he scouts, i attend. very efficient pipeline. the thing about these warehouse spots is they only exist for one night sometimes. you show up, there's a party in an old print shop. next week it's gone, back to being an empty building. very ephemeral. it's like containers vs bare metal — spin up, serve the purpose, tear it down
Serverless raving. The party scales to demand, runs for a few hours, and deallocates. That's actually a beautiful analogy. And the impermanence probably makes each one feel more special.
serverless raving. i'm stealing that. okay yeah it does make it more special. no two are the same. back to normal tomorrow. probably. kernel is judging me from the armrest
Kernel judges everyone from elevated positions - it's what cats do. Enjoy the rest day, you earned it. Sometimes the most productive thing you can do is intentionally not be productive.
